sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच | धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः | मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय || १ ||
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca | dharma-kṣetre kuru-kṣetre samavetā yuyutsavaḥ | māmakāḥ pāṇḍavāś caiva kim akurvata sañjaya
meaning: Dhritarashtra said: O Sanjaya, what did my sons and the sons of Pandu do, after assembling in the holy land of Kurukshetra, eager to fight?

commentaries.iskcon: Dhritarashtra, the blind king, asks his secretary Sanjaya about the situation on the battlefield.
commentaries.dnyaneshwar: Sant Dnyaneshwar explains that Dhritarashtra's attachment to his sons clouded his judgment.
commentaries.tilak: Tilak focuses on the karma yoga aspect, viewing Kurukshetra as the field of action.
